<content>1: /**
2:  * ProseMirror editor setup for collaborative document editing.
3:  * Uses prosemirror-example-setup for baseline functionality.
4:  * 
5:  * Supports multiple content modes:
6:  * - "rich" / "markdown" / "plain" - Full editor with toolbar
7:  * - "raw" - Minimal editor for code/config (no toolbar, no formatting shortcuts)
8:  * - "media" / "binary" - Not editable (handled elsewhere)
9:  */
10: 
11: import { EditorState, type Transaction, type Plugin, TextSelection } from 'prosemirror-state';
12: import { EditorView } from 'prosemirror-view';
13: import { Schema, DOMParser, Node } from 'prosemirror-model';
14: import { schema as basicSchema } from 'prosemirror-schema-basic';
15: import { addListNodes } from 'prosemirror-schema-list';
16: import { exampleSetup, buildMenuItems } from 'prosemirror-example-setup';
17: import { collab, sendableSteps, getVersion } from 'prosemirror-collab';
18: import { keymap } from 'prosemirror-keymap';
19: import { baseKeymap } from 'prosemirror-commands';
20: import { history } from 'prosemirror-history';
21: import { dropCursor } from 'prosemirror-dropcursor';
22: import { gapCursor } from 'prosemirror-gapcursor';
23: import { undoItem, redoItem, blockTypeItem, icons } from 'prosemirror-menu';
24: import { createCursorPlugin, type SendCursorFn } from './cursors';
25: 
26: /**
27:  * Content mode types matching server-side enum.
28:  * Determines how the editor is configured.
29:  */
30: export type ContentMode = 'rich' | 'markdown' | 'plain' | 'raw' | 'media' | 'binary';
31: 
32: /**
33:  * Full schema with list support for rich/markdown/plain modes.
34:  */
35: export const richSchema = new Schema({
36:   nodes: addListNodes(basicSchema.spec.nodes, 'paragraph block*', 'block'),
37:   marks: basicSchema.spec.marks,
38: });
39: 
40: /**
41:  * Minimal schema for raw mode (code/config files).
42:  * Only allows doc containing code_block nodes with text.
43:  * No marks (formatting) allowed.
44:  */
45: export const rawSchema = new Schema({
46:   nodes: {
47:     doc: { content: 'code_block+' },
48:     text: { group: 'inline' },
49:     code_block: {
50:       content: 'text*',
51:       marks: '',
52:       group: 'block',
53:       code: true,
54:       defining: true,
55:       parseDOM: [{ tag: 'pre', preserveWhitespace: 'full' }],
56:       toDOM() { return ['pre', ['code', 0]]; },
57:     },
58:   },
59:   marks: {},
60: });
61: 
62: // Export both schemas and a helper to get the right one
63: export { richSchema as schema };
64: 
65: /**
66:  * Get the schema for a content mode.
67:  */
68: export function getSchema(mode: ContentMode): Schema {
69:   return mode === 'raw' ? rawSchema : richSchema;
70: }
71: 
72: /**
73:  * Check if a mode uses the full editor with toolbar.
74:  */
75: export function hasToolbar(mode: ContentMode): boolean {
76:   return mode === 'rich' || mode === 'markdown' || mode === 'plain';
77: }
78: 
79: /**
80:  * Check if a mode is editable.
81:  */
82: export function isEditable(mode: ContentMode): boolean {
83:   return mode !== 'media' && mode !== 'binary';
84: }
85: 
86: export interface EditorInstance {
87:   view: EditorView;
88:   schema: Schema;
89:   clientID: number;
90:   mode: ContentMode;
91: }
92: 
93: export interface CollabState {
94:   version: number;
95:   unconfirmed: Transaction[];
96: }
97: 
98: /**
99:  * Initialize a ProseMirror editor in the given container.
100:  * 
101:  * @param container - The DOM element to mount the editor in
102:  * @param initialDoc - Optional initial document as ProseMirror JSON
103:  * @param collabVersion - Starting version for collaboration (default 0)
104:  * @param mode - Content mode determining schema and plugins
105:  * @param sendCursor - Optional callback to send cursor updates
106:  * @returns The editor instance
107:  */
108: export function initEditor(
109:   container: HTMLElement,
110:   initialDoc?: unknown,
111:   collabVersion: number = 0,
112:   mode: ContentMode = 'raw',
113:   sendCursor?: SendCursorFn
114: ): EditorInstance {
115:   console.log('[editor] initEditor called with mode:', mode, 'collabVersion:', collabVersion);
116:   console.log('[editor] initialDoc:', initialDoc ? JSON.stringify(initialDoc).slice(0, 300) : 'undefined');
117:   
118:   // Generate a random client ID for this session
119:   const clientID = Math.floor(Math.random() * 0xFFFFFFFF);
120:   
121:   // Select schema based on mode
122:   const editorSchema = getSchema(mode);
123:   console.log('[editor] Using schema for mode:', mode, 'hasToolbar:', hasToolbar(mode));
124:   
125:   // Parse initial document from JSON if provided, otherwise create empty
126:   let doc: Node;
127:   if (initialDoc && typeof initialDoc === 'object') {
128:     try {
129:       console.log('[editor] Parsing initialDoc with Node.fromJSON');
130:       doc = Node.fromJSON(editorSchema, initialDoc);
131:       console.log('[editor] Parsed doc successfully, content:', doc.toString().slice(0, 200));
132:     } catch (err) {
133:       console.error('[editor] Failed to parse initial doc JSON, using empty doc:', err);
134:       doc = editorSchema.topNodeType.createAndFill() ?? editorSchema.node('doc');
135:     }
136:   } else {
137:     console.log('[editor] No initialDoc, creating empty doc');
138:     doc = editorSchema.topNodeType.createAndFill() ?? editorSchema.node('doc');
139:   }
140: 
141:   // Build plugins list based on mode
142:   const plugins: Plugin[] = [];
143:   
144:   if (hasToolbar(mode)) {
145:     // Build custom menu that flattens the Type dropdown into inline buttons
146:     const menuItems = buildMenuItems(editorSchema);
147:     
148:     // Filter out null/undefined items
149:     const cut = <T>(arr: (T | null | undefined)[]): T[] => arr.filter((x): x is T => x != null);
150:     
151:     // Create compact block type items with short labels
152:     const paragraph = editorSchema.nodes.paragraph;
153:     const codeBlock = editorSchema.nodes.code_block;
154:     const heading = editorSchema.nodes.heading;
155:     
156:     const makeParagraph = paragraph && blockTypeItem(paragraph, {
157:       title: 'Change to paragraph',
158:       label: '¶',
159:     });
160:     const makeCodeBlock = codeBlock && blockTypeItem(codeBlock, {
161:       title: 'Change to code block',
162:       label: '</>',
163:     });
164:     
165:     // Create heading items H1-H6 with compact labels
166:     const makeHeadings = heading ? [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6].map(level => 
167:       blockTypeItem(heading, {
168:         title: `Change to heading ${level}`,
169:         label: `H${level}`,
170:         attrs: { level },
171:       })
172:     ) : [];
173:     
174:     // Build flattened menu structure:
175:     // Row 1: inline formatting (bold, italic, code, link)
176:     // Row 2: block types (paragraph, code, H1-H6) + undo/redo
177:     // Row 3: lists, blockquote, structure tools
178:     const customMenu = [
179:       // Inline formatting
180:       cut([menuItems.toggleStrong, menuItems.toggleEm, menuItems.toggleCode, menuItems.toggleLink]),
181:       // Block types flattened + undo/redo
182:       cut([
183:         makeParagraph,
184:         makeCodeBlock,
185:         ...makeHeadings,
186:         undoItem,
187:         redoItem,
188:       ]),
189:       // Block structure tools
190:       cut([
191:         menuItems.wrapBulletList,
192:         menuItems.wrapOrderedList,
193:         menuItems.wrapBlockQuote,
194:         menuItems.liftItem,
195:         menuItems.selectParentNodeItem,
196:       ]),
197:     ];
198:     
199:     plugins.push(...exampleSetup({ 
200:       schema: editorSchema, 
201:       menuContent: customMenu,
202:     }));
203:   } else {
204:     // Minimal setup for raw mode - just basic editing, no menu/toolbar
205:     plugins.push(
206:       history(),
207:       dropCursor(),
208:       gapCursor(),
209:       keymap(baseKeymap),
210:     );
211:   }
212:   
213:   // Always add collab plugin
214:   plugins.push(collab({ version: collabVersion, clientID }));
215:   
216:   // Add cursor plugin if sendCursor callback provided
217:   if (sendCursor) {
218:     plugins.push(createCursorPlugin(clientID, sendCursor));
219:   }
220: 
221:   // Create editor state
222:   const state = EditorState.create({
223:     doc,
224:     plugins,
225:   });
226: 
227:   // Add mode-specific CSS class
228:   const editorClass = hasToolbar(mode) ? 'id-editor id-editor-rich' : 'id-editor id-editor-raw';
229: 
230:   // Create editor view
231:   const view = new EditorView(container, {
232:     state,
233:     dispatchTransaction(transaction: Transaction) {
234:       const newState = view.state.apply(transaction);
235:       view.updateState(newState);
236:       
237:       // Dispatch custom event for collab sync
238:       // Only for LOCAL changes - remote changes have 'addToHistory' set to false by receiveTransaction
239:       // We also check that the transaction wasn't created by the collab plugin itself
240:       const isLocalChange = transaction.docChanged && 
241:         transaction.getMeta('addToHistory') !== false;
242:       
243:       if (isLocalChange) {
244:         console.log('[editor] Local document change, dispatching editor:change event');
245:         const event = new CustomEvent('editor:change', {
246:           detail: { transaction, state: newState },
247:           bubbles: true,
248:         });
249:         container.dispatchEvent(event);
250:       } else if (transaction.docChanged) {
251:         console.log('[editor] Remote document change (from collab), not dispatching event');
252:       }
253:     },
254:     handleKeyDown(view, event) {
255:       // Fix for cursor jumping 2 lines when pressing Up at start of visual line.
256:       // The browser's native caret can be positioned at end of previous line
257:       // (visually same as start of current line), causing Up to move from there.
258:       // We detect this case and manually compute the correct target position.
259:       if (event.key === 'ArrowUp') {
260:         const { $head } = view.state.selection;
261:         
262:         // Check if we're at the start of a visual line:
263:         // - parentOffset is 0 (start of block), OR
264:         // - character before cursor is a newline
265:         const textBefore = $head.parent.textContent.slice(0, $head.parentOffset);
266:         const isAtVisualLineStart = $head.parentOffset === 0 || textBefore.endsWith('\n');
267:         
268:         if (isAtVisualLineStart) {
269:           // Get current visual coordinates
270:           const coords = view.coordsAtPos($head.pos);
271:           const lineHeight = coords.bottom - coords.top;
272:           
273:           // Move up by half a line height to ensure we're in the previous line
274:           const targetY = coords.top - lineHeight / 2;
275:           const targetPos = view.posAtCoords({ left: coords.left, top: targetY });
276:           
277:           if (targetPos && targetPos.pos < $head.pos) {
278:             const tr = view.state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(view.state.doc, targetPos.pos));
279:             view.dispatch(tr);
280:             return true; // Handled
281:           }
282:         }
283:       }
284:       return false; // Let ProseMirror/browser handle it
285:     },
286:     attributes: {
287:       class: editorClass,
288:       spellcheck: 'false',
289:     },
290:   });
291: 
292:   return { view, schema: editorSchema, clientID, mode };
293: }
294: 
295: /**
296:  * Get the current state suitable for serialization.
297:  */
298: export function getEditorState(view: EditorView): {
299:   version: number;
300:   doc: unknown;
301:   steps: unknown[] | null;
302: } {
303:   const state = view.state;
304:   const sendable = sendableSteps(state);
305:   
306:   return {
307:     version: getVersion(state),
308:     doc: state.doc.toJSON(),
309:     steps: sendable ? sendable.steps.map(s => s.toJSON()) : null,
310:   };
311: }
312: 
313: /**
314:  * Get sendable steps from the editor for transmission to server.
315:  */
316: export function getSendableSteps(view: EditorView): {
317:   version: number;
318:   steps: unknown[];
319:   clientID: string | number;
320: } | null {
321:   const sendable = sendableSteps(view.state);
322:   if (!sendable) return null;
323:   
324:   return {
325:     version: getVersion(view.state),
326:     steps: sendable.steps.map(s => s.toJSON()),
327:     clientID: sendable.clientID,
328:   };
329: }
330: 
331: export { getVersion };

(End of file - total 331 lines)
